---
title: "Lecture 01 PDF Notes"
subtitle: "Printable quant handout"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    mainfont: "NotoSerifCJK-Regular.ttc"
    mainfontoptions:
      - "Path=/usr/share/fonts/opentype/noto/"
      - "FontIndex=2"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSerifCJK-Bold.ttc"
    sansfont: "NotoSansCJK-Regular.ttc"
    sansfontoptions:
      - "Path=/usr/share/fonts/opentype/noto/"
      - "FontIndex=2"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSansCJK-Bold.ttc"
    monofont: "MapleMono-NF-CN-Regular.ttf"
    monofontoptions:
      - "Path=../assets/fonts/"
      - "BoldFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Bold.ttf"
      - "ItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Italic.ttf"
      - "BoldItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-BoldItalic.ttf"
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
include-in-header:
  text: |
    \addtokomafont{disposition}{\rmfamily\bfseries}
    \addtokomafont{title}{\rmfamily\bfseries}
    \addtokomafont{author}{\rmfamily}
    \addtokomafont{date}{\rmfamily}
---

# Role Of The PDF

Use the PDF output when you want:

- an offline handout
- something that reads more like notes than slides
- easy annotation during study or review

# Minimal Content Pattern

Each lecture can keep the same section order:

1. Objective
2. Signal definition
3. Risk model
4. Backtest protocol
5. Main chart set
6. Failure analysis

# Example Equation

$$
\text{Turnover}_t = |w_t - w_{t-1}|
$$

$$
c_t = \alpha \cdot \text{Turnover}_t + \beta \cdot \sigma_t
$$

The point is not the exact model here.
The point is that Quarto gives you a clean math-capable path to a printable handout.

# Figure Placement

For PDF handouts, prefer raster or PDF-native figures when you want zero extra system dependencies.

Example placeholders:

- cumulative PnL chart generated to `png` by your Python pipeline
- return histogram generated to `png`
- drawdown chart generated to `png`

\newpage

```python
fig_pnl.savefig("figures/pnl.png", dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
fig_hist.savefig("figures/returns-hist.png", dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
```

Then include them in the PDF handout with:

```markdown
![](figures/pnl.png){width="90%"}
![](figures/returns-hist.png){width="80%"}
```

# Notes

This file is intentionally simple.
For a real course, you would likely include:

- brief derivations
- parameter tables
- benchmark comparisons
- reading references
